<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.3) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.7) — 1 imports target "web-23"
- High coupling (0.7) — 1 imports target "polling-lifecycle-management"
- Integrates memory tracking utility with React hooks and warning components
- Serves as early warning system for memory pressure in long-running viewer sessions
- Provides both programmatic monitoring and user-visible alerts
- Low cohesion (0.3) and high coupling (0.7) suggest this zone may need architectural refactoring to improve module boundaries
- External dependency on polling infrastructure indicates timing services are foundational to monitoring architecture
- Depends on external polling-lifecycle-management zone, creating cross-zone coupling that contributes to high coupling metrics
- Violates architectural layering by mixing utility (memory-monitor.ts), React hook (use-memory-monitor.ts), and UI component (memory-warning.ts) in single zone
- Contrasts with dom-performance-monitoring zone which properly separates these concerns
- Architectural layering violation: zone mixes utility logic, React state management, and UI presentation in single module
- High-risk zone with both low cohesion (0.3) and high coupling (0.7), making it architecturally fragile and prone to breakage during refactoring
- Split into three focused zones following dom-performance-monitoring pattern: memory-monitor (utility), memory-hooks (React integration), memory-ui (warning component)
- Zone represents high architectural risk due to combined low cohesion and high coupling metrics - prioritize refactoring
- [call graph] 110 internal calls, 1 outgoing, 80 incoming (cohesion: 0.99, coupling: 0.01)
